channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 4 American soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have died, NATO leader says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, but that he did not yet know the details. A U.S. official would say only that the four soldiers were involved in a training accident.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US soldiers missing in Lithuania: Pentagon
item.description: The Army said Wednesday that four U.S. soldiers are missing in Lithuania. The U.S. Army Europe and Africa said in a press release that the missing soldiers, part of the Army’s 1st Brigade, 3rd Infantry Division, were close to the Lithuanian city of Pabradė within a training area. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: At least 24 dead as wildfires ravage southern South Korea and force thousands to evacuate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Wind-driven wildfires that were among South Korea’s worst ever have ravaged the country’s southern regions, killing 24 people, destroying more than 300 structures and forcing 28,800 residents to evacuate, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices has unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Accuser tells a trial that actor Gérard Depardieu groped her bottom and breasts on film set
item.description: A woman accusing French actor Gérard Depardieu of sexual assault has told a landmark trial in Paris that he groped her buttocks and her breasts several times on a film set.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Captured Ukrainians convicted on terrorism charges by Russia in what Kyiv called a sham trial
item.description: Russia has convicted 23 captured Ukrainians on terrorism charges stemming from the war in Ukraine in a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thai prime minister survives a challenge by rivals who say her father is pulling the strings
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra easily survived a no-confidence vote in Parliament on Wednesday, following a two-day debate in which rivals charged that she has mismanaged the country and let her father, a former prime minister, control her administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey's opposition leader visits jailed Erdogan challenger Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — The head of Turkey's main opposition party visited jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu on Tuesday after six nights of massive protests calling for his release.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds in Gaza take to streets to protest Hamas
item.description: Hundreds of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip took to the streets Tuesday for a rare protest against the Palestinian militant group Hamas, which has controlled the war-torn strip for nearly two decades.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Russia, Ukraine agree to ‘eliminate the use of force’ in Black Sea
item.description: The White House announced Tuesday it came to an agreement with Ukraine and Russia to “eliminate the use of force” in the Black Sea, as well as end strikes on energy facilities, marking one of the more significant steps the Trump administration has seen toward a stop in fighting.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: How a shelter hit by Trump's aid cuts protects LGBTQ migrants in Mexico
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — Ana Esquivel no longer feels like her heart stops every time she sees a police officer.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, UN envoy says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — South Sudan is teetering on the edge of renewed civil war, the top U.N. official in the world’s youngest nation warned on Monday, lamenting the government’s sudden postponement of the latest peace effort.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Buttigieg pushes for accountability after war plans chat breach
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg on Monday pushed for accountability in the wake of The Atlantic Editor-in-Chief Jeffrey Goldberg being added to a group chat in which top Trump administration officials talked about an attack on Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the reinstatement of South Korea's No. 2 leader and the Yoon ruling to come
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In the latest twist in South Korea’s ongoing political crisis, the country’s Constitutional Court reinstated Prime Minister Han Duck-soo as acting leader, overturning his impeachment by opposition lawmakers three months ago.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US peace talks with Ukraine, Russia get underway in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Discussions between U.S. and Russian delegations aimed at ending the war in Ukraine are underway Monday in Saudi Arabia, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Christian watch group rises up to protect community amid growing violence in Syria
item.description: The Eagles of Antioch is a group of volunteers who stand watch over Christian neighborhoods in Damascus, protecting shrines, icons and churches from vandalism and attacks in post-Assad Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US ranked 24th among happiest countries in the world: Research
item.description: The United States is still one of the most desired countries to live in, but its residents may not be the happiest.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces push deeper into Gaza and destroy its only cancer hospital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces advanced deeper into the Gaza Strip on Friday and blew up the only specialized cancer hospital in the war-torn territory, as Israeli leaders vowed to capture more land until Hamas releases its remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: IBRAHIM HAZBOUN, SAM MEDNICK and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK issues travel warning for US
item.description: The United Kingdom updated its advice, warning of harsh consequences for British passport holders who violate U.S. immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Heathrow says some flights will resume after a fire cut power to Europe's busiest airport
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heathrow Airport said it planned to resume some flights Friday after a large fire at an electrical substation knocked out power to Europe’s busiest flight hub and disrupted global travel for hundreds of thousands of passengers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, DANICA KIRKA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: European countries warn against US travel amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The UK and Germany updated travel advisories after citizens were arrested and detained when attempting to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel returns to war in Gaza with wider aims and almost no constraints
item.description: Israel's renewed military offensive in the Gaza Strip threatens to be even deadlier and more destructive than the last, as it pursues wider aims with far fewer constraints.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Strikes in Gaza kill 85 overnight, bringing the total since Israel broke ceasefire to nearly 600
item.description: DEIR-AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Local health officials said Israeli strikes killed at least 85 Palestinians across he Gaza Strip overnight and into Thursday, bringing the total to nearly 600 killed since Israel shattered a truce that had facilitated the release of more than two dozen hostages and b
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Finland is again ranked the happiest country in the world. The US falls to its lowest-ever position
item.description: HELSINKI (AP) — Finland is the happiest country in the world for the eighth year in a row, according to the World Happiness Report 2025 published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and KOSTYA MANENKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taliban release an American man who was abducted while traveling in Afghanistan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American man who was abducted more than two years ago while traveling through Afghanistan as a tourist has been released by the Taliban in a deal with the Trump administration that Qatari negotiators helped broker, the State Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US offers $15M reward for team accused of smuggling drone tech to Iran
item.description: Liu Baoxia, Li Yongxin, Yung Yiu Wa and Zhong Yanlai are accused of facilitating the sale of U.S. goods to front companies based in China.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli forces are recapturing a key corridor that cuts Gaza in half
item.description: The Israeli military is retaking control of a key corridor that cuts off northern Gaza from the south, Israel's military said, deepening its renewed offensive in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
